steps: Run a 50 µL reaction instead of 20 µL .

1. Prepare Master Mix – Number of reactions + 1 (as extra).2. For each PCR tube: 2.5 µL DNA sample + 47 µL Master Mix.3. Bio-Rad Thermal Cycler – Select/Edit Protocol (see PCR Cycle below). Master Mix – for one 50 µL reaction : %s %s PCR Cycle : %s %s Annealing temperature should be 5 °C - 10 °C lower than Tm of primers.
Source: Qiagen MinElute Gel Extraction Kit (modified) Run gel electrophoresis as instructed above.
Excise the desired gel band under UV light.
Transfer the gel slice into a 1.5 mL tube.
Incubate at 50 °C until gel has completely dissolved. Mix the tube every 00:01:00 - 00:02:00 to help dissolve the gel.
Add 100 µL 100% isopropanol & mix by inverting.
Transfer sample to a MinElute spin column (stored at 4 °C ). Centrifuge at 8000 rpm, , 1 minute . Discard the filtrate. Put back the spin column to the same collection tube.
Repeat step 9 until all sample has passed through the column.
Add 650 µL Buffer PE to spin column. Centrifuge at 8000 rpm, , 1 minute . Discard the filtrate. Put back the spin column to the same collection tube.
Centrifuge again at 13000 rpm, , 1 minute . Discard the filtrate & collection tube.
Place the column in a new 1.5 mL tube.
Add 35 µL - 50 µL Buffer EB. Make sure it is added to the center of the membrane.
Repeat step 14-16 to increase product yield. Reload with the DNA product at step 16 instead of adding new Buffer EB.
creator: Claire D Clelland
